name: VibecodeApp PRD - Aider-style repository architecture map
description: Product requirements for the initial VibecodeApp implementation: a local, deterministic, agent-friendly repository architecture map and context engine. The goal is not to build a new coding agent or an OpenCode runtime. The goal is to create a .vibecode project layer that can initialize a project, index files, symbols, imports, tests, entrypoints, risky areas, human-confirmed invariants, and generate a short context pack for a specific task. This PRD is written for a Ralph/Ralphy loop: tasks are intentionally sequential, verifiable, and free of unnecessary design detours. Do not label this scope with a numbered release name.

tasks.title: 01 - Lock the implementation scope and non-goals
tasks.description:
Create a clear implementation boundary document for the initial architecture-map scope.

Scope:
- implement only the Vibecode architecture map and context engine,
- do not implement a custom coding agent,
- do not implement a GUI,
- do not implement an MCP server,
- do not implement an OpenCode run adapter,
- do not implement auto-commit or auto-approve behavior.

Required artifacts:
- docs/PRD_VIBECODE_ARCHITECTURE_MAP.md or a similarly named document,
- a short Non-goals section in the README or developer documentation if a README already exists.

Acceptance criteria:
- the document clearly says this initial scope ends at indexing, mapping, context-pack generation, and OpenCode prompt export,
- the document warns that agent runtime work comes only after the map is working,
- no code in this phase launches OpenCode or any other agent.

Verification:
- the document contains these sections: Scope, Non-goals, Generated vs human-maintained files, Acceptance criteria.

tasks.title: 02 - Create the base Python package skeleton for the vibecode CLI
tasks.description:
Create a minimal Python package for a CLI tool named `vibecode`.

Required structure:
- vibecode/__init__.py
- vibecode/cli.py
- vibecode/project.py
- vibecode/config.py
- vibecode/indexer/__init__.py
- vibecode/context/__init__.py
- tests/ or test_vibecode_*.py according to the repository style.

The CLI must support at least:
- `python -m vibecode.cli --help`
- `python -m vibecode.cli init --help`
- `python -m vibecode.cli index --help`
- `python -m vibecode.cli context --help`
- `python -m vibecode.cli map --help`

Acceptance criteria:
- all help commands exit successfully,
- the CLI uses only the local filesystem,
- if the repository already has dependency management, integrate naturally; otherwise keep it runnable through `python -m vibecode.cli`.

Verification:
- `python -m vibecode.cli --help` returns exit code 0,
- `python -c "import vibecode"` succeeds.

tasks.title: 03 - Implement init and create the .vibecode structure
tasks.description:
Implement `vibecode init <repo_root> --id <project_id> --name <project_name>`.

The command creates this minimum structure in the target repository:
- .vibecode/project.yaml
- .vibecode/index/
- .vibecode/architecture/INVARIANTS.md
- .vibecode/architecture/STRUCTURE.md
- .vibecode/architecture/MODULE_BOUNDARIES.md
- .vibecode/architecture/PROTECTED_AREAS.md
- .vibecode/current/
- .vibecode/handoff/NOW.md
- .vibecode/handoff/NEXT.md
- .vibecode/handoff/BLOCKERS.md
- .vibecode/history/README.md
- .vibecode/logs/index_runs/

project.yaml must contain at least:
- config schema marker,
- project.id,
- project.name,
- project.root,
- indexing.include,
- indexing.exclude,
- protected_paths,
- risk_rules,
- required_checks.

Acceptance criteria:
- init is idempotent: repeated runs do not damage existing human-maintained files,
- if a file exists, do not overwrite it without explicit `--force`,
- generated directories may be created empty,
- paths work on Windows and Unix-like systems.

Verification:
- run init on a temporary test repository,
- verify all required files and directories exist,
- run init a second time and verify a manually edited INVARIANTS.md was not overwritten.

tasks.title: 04 - Implement loading and validation for project.yaml
tasks.description:
Implement the configuration layer for `.vibecode/project.yaml`.

Requirements:
- load project identity,
- load include/exclude glob rules,
- load protected_paths,
- load risk_rules,
- load required_checks,
- normalize the root path,
- return clear errors for missing or invalid config.

Technical note:
- YAML is the user-facing format. If the project does not already use PyYAML, either add the dependency cleanly or implement a narrow fallback for the generated format only. Do not introduce a large framework.

Acceptance criteria:
- a valid project.yaml loads into a ProjectConfig data structure,
- missing mandatory fields produce readable errors,
- Windows paths using backslashes or slashes are normalized safely,
- the config loader does not mutate the config file.

Verification:
- unit test for a valid config,
- unit test for missing project.id,
- unit test for a nonexistent root,
- unit test for a Windows-style path.

tasks.title: 05 - Implement safe file scanning through git ls-files with filesystem fallback
tasks.description:
Implement an indexer that collects the repository file list.

Requirements:
- prefer `git ls-files` when the target is a git repository,
- fall back to filesystem walking when git is unavailable or the target is not a git repository,
- distinguish tracked/untracked files at a basic level when git is available,
- never index `.git/`, `node_modules/`, `.venv/`, `venv/`, `__pycache__/`, `dist/`, `build/`, `.pytest_cache/`, `.mypy_cache/`, cache directories, or binary/large artifacts excluded by rules,
- respect include/exclude rules from project.yaml.

Acceptance criteria:
- the scan does not enter node_modules or .venv,
- returned paths are relative POSIX-style paths from the repository root,
- scanning works without git,
- files above a reasonable size limit are skipped or marked according to a clear strategy instead of being pushed into the map.

Verification:
- test with a temporary git repository,
- test without git,
- test exclude rules,
- test that `.git/` and `node_modules/` do not appear in results.

tasks.title: 06 - Implement language detection and role_guess for file_inventory
tasks.description:
Implement deterministic language detection and a rough file role guess.

Minimum languages:
- python: .py
- typescript: .ts
- typescriptreact: .tsx
- javascript: .js
- javascriptreact: .jsx
- markdown: .md
- json: .json
- yaml: .yml/.yaml
- toml: .toml
- config/unknown as needed.

Minimum role guesses:
- backend_engine
- backend_api
- frontend_screen
- frontend_component
- test
- doc
- config
- script
- generated
- unknown.

Heuristics:
- `tests/**`, `test_*.py`, `*_test.py`, `*.test.tsx`, `*.spec.tsx` => test,
- `docs/**`, `README.md`, `*.md` => doc,
- `ui/frontend/src/screens/**` => frontend_screen,
- `ui/frontend/src/components/**` => frontend_component,
- `app/engine/**` or similar engine paths => backend_engine,
- `api/**`, `routes/**`, `server/**` => backend_api,
- config names such as pyproject.toml, package.json, tsconfig.json, vite.config.* => config.

Acceptance criteria:
- every FileRecord has path, language, size_bytes, role_guess, is_test, is_config, is_doc, risk_level,
- heuristics are deterministic,
- unknown file types never crash indexing.

Verification:
- unit tests for typical Python, TSX, Markdown, config, and test files.

tasks.title: 07 - Generate .vibecode/index/file_inventory.json
tasks.description:
Generate `file_inventory.json` from file scanning and metadata detection.

The JSON format must include:
- schema marker,
- project_id,
- generated_at ISO timestamp,
- root,
- files[].

Each file record includes:
- path,
- language,
- size_bytes,
- role_guess,
- is_test,
- is_config,
- is_doc,
- risk_level,
- tracked when the information is available.

Acceptance criteria:
- the file is valid JSON,
- generated_at uses a timezone-aware timestamp or explicit UTC ISO format,
- paths are relative and stable,
- repeated indexing updates generated files without touching human-maintained files.

Verification:
- `python -m json.tool .vibecode/index/file_inventory.json` succeeds,
- a test checks at least two files of different types.

tasks.title: 08 - Generate compact .vibecode/index/repo_tree.md
tasks.description:
Implement a renderer for `repo_tree.md`.

Content:
- heading: Repo tree,
- project root,
- top-level folders with short role notes,
- important subfolders according to role_guess,
- highlight high/medium risk files,
- omit excluded/generated/cache folders,
- the tree must not become an endless dump of the entire repository.

Acceptance criteria:
- repo_tree.md is readable for humans and agents,
- it includes the main folders,
- it does not show node_modules, .venv, or .git,
- high-risk files are marked `[HIGH RISK]` when present,
- large repositories still produce a compact tree.

Verification:
- snapshot or text test over a fixture repository,
- manual review that the tree is not a blind `tree /F` dump.

tasks.title: 09 - Implement Python symbol extraction through ast
tasks.description:
Implement `extract_python_symbols(path)` using the standard Python `ast` module.

Detect at minimum:
- class definitions,
- functions,
- async functions,
- methods as function/method based on parent class when practical,
- FastAPI-like route handlers from decorators such as `@app.get`, `@router.post`, `@router.put`, `@router.delete`, and `@router.patch`.

Symbol record:
- path,
- name,
- kind,
- line_start,
- line_end when available.

Acceptance criteria:
- parser does not crash on normal Python files,
- syntax errors in one file are written as warnings and do not stop the whole index,
- route handlers are marked as `api_route` when a supported decorator is detected,
- output is stable.

Verification:
- unit test for class/function/async function,
- unit test for a FastAPI decorator,
- unit test for a syntactically broken file that does not stop indexing.

tasks.title: 10 - Implement heuristic TypeScript and TSX symbol extraction
tasks.description:
Implement a rough but useful symbol extractor for .ts/.tsx/.js/.jsx without a heavy compiler integration.

Detect at minimum:
- `export function Name`,
- `function Name`,
- `export const Name =`,
- `const Name =`,
- `export interface Name`,
- `interface Name`,
- `export type Name =`,
- `type Name =`,
- React components heuristically as PascalCase function/const declarations in .tsx/.jsx.

Acceptance criteria:
- extractor does not crash on TSX JSX syntax,
- it returns line_start and kind,
- if line_end is not reliable, use null,
- perfection is not expected; the goal is rough orientation for the initial implementation.

Verification:
- unit test with a TSX component,
- unit test with a `useSomething` hook,
- unit test with interface/type declarations,
- unit test that plain CSS/JSON is not parsed as TypeScript symbols.

tasks.title: 11 - Generate .vibecode/index/symbol_map.json
tasks.description:
Combine the Python and TypeScript/TSX extractors into `symbol_map.json`.

Format:
- schema marker,
- generated_at,
- files[] with path, language, and symbols[].

Acceptance criteria:
- output is valid JSON,
- Python symbols are available,
- TS/TSX symbols are available at least heuristically,
- files without symbols are either omitted or included with an empty list according to a documented strategy,
- parsing errors are logged into the index run log instead of being hidden.

Verification:
- `python -m json.tool .vibecode/index/symbol_map.json` succeeds,
- a fixture test includes Python and TSX files and both appear in the output.

tasks.title: 12 - Implement initial import and dependency extraction
tasks.description:
Implement a simple import map for Python and TypeScript/JavaScript.

Python:
- `import x`,
- `from x import y`.

TS/JS:
- `import ... from '...'`,
- `import '...'`,
- optional `require('...')`.

The goal is not a complete resolver. The goal is an edge map showing that file A depends on module/import B.

Output `dependency_map.json`:
- schema marker,
- generated_at,
- edges[] with from, to or import_target, type, and resolved_path when it can be safely resolved inside the repository.

Acceptance criteria:
- import extraction does not crash on unknown imports,
- relative TS/Python imports attempt to resolve to repository paths,
- external dependencies are marked external/unresolved,
- output is valid JSON.

Verification:
- unit test for Python import,
- unit test for relative TS import,
- unit test for external import.

tasks.title: 13 - Implement test discovery and test_map.json
tasks.description:
Implement test discovery and rough mapping from source files to tests.

Detection:
- Python: `tests/**`, `test_*.py`, `*_test.py`, files containing pytest/unittest patterns,
- TS/React: `*.test.ts`, `*.test.tsx`, `*.spec.ts`, `*.spec.tsx`, Playwright, Cypress when present.

Mapping:
- `foo.py` -> `test_foo.py`, `foo_test.py`, tests importing foo,
- `component.tsx` -> `component.test.tsx`, `component.spec.tsx`, related screen tests by name,
- manual required_checks from project.yaml take precedence.

Output `test_map.json`:
- schema marker,
- generated_at,
- tests[],
- rules[] with path_pattern, required_checks, and reason.

Acceptance criteria:
- test_map is valid JSON,
- a known source file has a recommended test when the fixture contains a matching test,
- required_checks from config are carried through,
- if no tests are found, output contains an empty list and a warning instead of crashing.

Verification:
- unit test for a Python source/test pair,
- unit test for a TSX source/test pair,
- test for config-level required_checks.

tasks.title: 14 - Generate entrypoints.md from configs and heuristics
tasks.description:
Implement generation of `.vibecode/index/entrypoints.md`.

Detect and describe at minimum:
- Python entrypoints: main.py, app.py, cli.py, FastAPI app bootstrap, scripts,
- frontend entrypoints: src/main.tsx, src/App.tsx, Vite config,
- package scripts from package.json,
- pyproject entrypoints/scripts when present,
- Docker/Makefile entrypoints when present.

Acceptance criteria:
- entrypoints.md exists after indexing,
- it contains sections for Backend, Frontend, CLI/Scripts, and Runtime/config according to available data,
- if an entrypoint is not detected, the section says `not detected` instead of inventing information,
- generated text does not claim anything unsupported by files/configs.

Verification:
- fixture with main.py and main.tsx,
- fixture with package.json scripts,
- test that nonexistent entrypoints are not hallucinated.

tasks.title: 15 - Implement risk and protected-area mapping
tasks.description:
Implement the risk engine for `risky_files.md` and risk_level in file inventory.

Sources:
- explicit `protected_paths` from project.yaml,
- `risk_rules` from project.yaml,
- filename heuristics: matching, policy, state, migration, auth, security, tax, fx, payments, permissions,
- docs/audit and architecture files are at least medium risk unless config says otherwise.

Output:
- `.vibecode/index/risky_files.md`,
- updated `risk_level` in file_inventory.json.

Acceptance criteria:
- explicit protected paths are always high risk or use the configured rule severity,
- risky_files.md explains the reason,
- high-risk files are mentioned in the context pack as protected/risky,
- empty protected_paths produce a warning.

Verification:
- unit test for explicit protected path,
- unit test for heuristic matching.py/policy.py,
- test that empty protected_paths creates a warning.

tasks.title: 16 - Create human-maintained architecture templates without destructive overwrite
tasks.description:
During init, create useful templates for human-maintained architecture files.

Files:
- `.vibecode/architecture/INVARIANTS.md`,
- `.vibecode/architecture/STRUCTURE.md`,
- `.vibecode/architecture/MODULE_BOUNDARIES.md`,
- `.vibecode/architecture/PROTECTED_AREAS.md`.

Requirements:
- init creates them only when they do not exist,
- repeated init/index does not overwrite them,
- templates clearly ask the human to fill in project rules,
- templates do not hallucinate domain rules the user did not provide.

Acceptance criteria:
- human files are separate from generated indexes,
- indexing does not modify them,
- empty/unfilled templates produce a validation warning.

Verification:
- test that a manually edited INVARIANTS.md survives init and index.

tasks.title: 17 - Implement index run log and last_index.json
tasks.description:
Each `vibecode index` run must write an auditable record.

Outputs:
- `.vibecode/current/last_index.json`,
- `.vibecode/logs/index_runs/<timestamp>.json`.

Content:
- project_id,
- root,
- started_at,
- finished_at,
- counts: files, symbols, tests, dependency_edges, warnings, errors,
- warnings[],
- errors[] for non-fatal errors,
- generator/build identifier when available.

Acceptance criteria:
- an index run log exists after every index,
- parsing warnings are discoverable,
- fatal errors exit non-zero with a readable message,
- non-fatal errors in one file do not stop the whole repository.

Verification:
- test with a syntactically broken Python file produces a warning in the log,
- last_index.json contains counts.

tasks.title: 18 - Implement validation command or validation phase
tasks.description:
Run validation after indexing. This may be a standalone `vibecode validate <repo_root>` command or an automatic phase of `index`.

Checks:
- .vibecode/project.yaml exists,
- root path exists,
- generated JSON files are valid,
- .git/node_modules/.venv are not in inventory,
- architecture/INVARIANTS.md exists,
- protected_paths is non-empty or warning is emitted,
- a dummy context pack can be generated,
- generated vs human-maintained write rules are respected.

Acceptance criteria:
- validation prints OK/WARN/ERROR items,
- warnings are not failures,
- errors are failures,
- validation result is saved into last_index.json or a validation report.

Verification:
- valid fixture exits 0,
- missing project.yaml exits non-zero,
- empty invariants produce a warning, not a failure.

tasks.title: 19 - Implement relevant-file scoring for a task
tasks.description:
Implement a simple deterministic scorer that selects relevant files for a task.

Base scoring:
- +10 when path/filename contains a task keyword,
- +8 when symbol name contains a task keyword,
- +6 when a file is mentioned in handoff/NOW.md,
- +5 when a file is relevant in history by simple keyword match,
- +4 when a file is a test for a selected source file,
- +3 when dependency_map connects the file with a selected module,
- -10 for excluded/generated files,
- -8 for protected files unless the task explicitly requires them.

Requirements:
- task tokenization is simple and stable,
- the scorer returns the reason for including each relevant file,
- protected files may appear as risky/not_allowed rather than normal edit targets unless the task explicitly signals them.

Acceptance criteria:
- a task such as `context panel copy` selects the context-panel screen/test when the fixture exists,
- a task such as `matching algorithm` marks matching.py as relevant high risk,
- scoring reasons are visible in the context pack or debug output.

Verification:
- unit tests with two or three fixture tasks.

tasks.title: 20 - Implement context pack renderer
tasks.description:
Implement `vibecode context <repo_root> --task "..."`, which creates `.vibecode/current/context_pack.md`.

The context pack must contain:
- Project,
- Current task,
- Must preserve / invariants,
- Relevant architecture,
- Relevant files with reasons,
- Risky/protected files not allowed or requiring confirmation,
- Required checks,
- Handoff required,
- Working rule: smallest task-relevant change, no unrelated refactor.

Requirements:
- the context pack must be short and sharp, not a novel,
- it must not paste entire long files,
- it must put invariants before technical detail,
- it must clearly mark protected areas.

Acceptance criteria:
- the file is created,
- it contains the task exactly as entered by the user,
- it contains invariants from INVARIANTS.md,
- it contains relevant files from the scorer,
- it contains required checks from the test map/config,
- it contains a handoff instruction.

Verification:
- fixture repository test,
- manual review of context_pack.md: an agent should know where to work and where not to work.

tasks.title: 21 - Add length limits and content prioritization to context packs
tasks.description:
Context packs must not grow without bound. Implement a length limit and prioritization.

Requirements:
- default word or character limit, for example 8,000 words or a reasonable character limit,
- highest priority: task, invariants, protected files, relevant files, required checks,
- lower priority: generated summaries, broad repo tree, history,
- if content is truncated, the context pack must say what was shortened.

Acceptance criteria:
- long invariants or large history do not break the context pack,
- the context pack never attaches the whole repository,
- Must preserve content is not truncated before lower-priority sections.

Verification:
- test with an extremely long history file,
- test that Must preserve remains present.

tasks.title: 22 - Implement OpenCode prompt export without launching OpenCode
tasks.description:
Implement `vibecode context <repo_root> --task "..." --platform opencode`, which creates `.vibecode/current/opencode_prompt.md`.

Wrapper content:
- `You are working inside a Vibecode-controlled repository.`,
- pre-edit instructions: read the task, read relevant files, confirm protected files, prefer narrow edits,
- post-edit instructions: summarize changed files, list tests run, update handoff when instructed,
- embedded context_pack.md.

Important:
- do not launch OpenCode,
- only prepare the prompt file.

Acceptance criteria:
- opencode_prompt.md is created,
- it contains the wrapper and the context pack,
- no subprocess launches any agent runtime,
- platform export can be extended later for codex/claude.

Verification:
- test that the prompt file exists,
- test that no agent runtime is called.

tasks.title: 23 - Implement AGENTS.md export as a generated artifact
tasks.description:
Implement optional export of agent instructions.

The command may be:
- `vibecode export-agents <repo_root>` or a flag on context/index.

Output:
- `AGENTS.md` in the root or `.vibecode/generated/AGENTS.generated.md`, according to the safer strategy.

Content:
- the repository is controlled by VibecodeApp,
- before work, read `.vibecode/current/context_pack.md`, `architecture/INVARIANTS.md`, `architecture/MODULE_BOUNDARIES.md`, and `handoff/NOW.md`,
- do not perform unrelated refactors,
- do not modify protected files without an explicit task,
- do not update README outside marked generated blocks,
- run required checks,
- update handoff.

Safety:
- if AGENTS.md already exists and is not Vibecode-managed, do not overwrite it without `--force` or markers,
- prefer marker blocks or a generated-file strategy.

Acceptance criteria:
- export is safe with an existing AGENTS.md,
- generated text is short,
- the main source of truth remains in `.vibecode/`, not AGENTS.md.

Verification:
- test without existing AGENTS.md,
- test with an existing AGENTS.md without markers: do not overwrite.

tasks.title: 24 - Implement map/inspect CLI output for quick project review
tasks.description:
Implement `vibecode map <repo_root>` or `vibecode inspect <repo_root>`.

Console output:
- Project id/name/root,
- detected languages,
- file count,
- symbol count,
- test count,
- high-risk file count,
- entrypoint count,
- last index timestamp,
- warnings from the latest index.

Acceptance criteria:
- output is short and readable,
- it does not dump huge JSON,
- when no index exists yet, it suggests running `vibecode index` without a stack trace,
- it works in a Windows terminal.

Verification:
- test after index,
- test without index.

tasks.title: 25 - Enforce the human-maintained vs generated file boundary
tasks.description:
Introduce clear write rules.

Human-maintained:
- .vibecode/project.yaml,
- .vibecode/architecture/INVARIANTS.md,
- .vibecode/architecture/MODULE_BOUNDARIES.md,
- .vibecode/architecture/PROTECTED_AREAS.md,
- .vibecode/handoff/NOW.md,
- .vibecode/handoff/NEXT.md,
- .vibecode/handoff/BLOCKERS.md.

Generated:
- .vibecode/index/*.json,
- .vibecode/index/*.md,
- .vibecode/current/context_pack.md,
- .vibecode/current/opencode_prompt.md,
- .vibecode/current/last_index.json,
- .vibecode/logs/index_runs/*.json.

Acceptance criteria:
- index never overwrites human-maintained files,
- init never overwrites human-maintained files without force,
- generated files may be safely overwritten,
- README/AGENTS export uses markers or a safe no-overwrite strategy.

Verification:
- test that manually edited INVARIANTS.md remains unchanged after index.

tasks.title: 26 - Add fixture repositories for indexer tests
tasks.description:
Create a small fixture repository inside tests.

The fixture must include:
- Python backend file with class/function/FastAPI route,
- Python test file,
- TSX frontend screen with React component,
- TSX test file,
- package.json with a test script,
- pyproject.toml or simple config,
- README.md,
- dummy node_modules/.venv directories that must not be indexed,
- a high-risk file such as engine/matching.py.

Acceptance criteria:
- fixture is small,
- it runs without internet,
- it does not require real npm install or a virtualenv,
- it tests the main indexer behavior.

Verification:
- the test suite uses the fixture for file inventory, symbol map, test map, and context pack.

tasks.title: 27 - Add unit tests for the main indexing modules
tasks.description:
Add tests for key modules.

Minimum coverage scenarios:
- config load valid/invalid,
- init creates structure and is idempotent,
- file scan respects exclude,
- language detection,
- role_guess,
- Python symbol extraction,
- TSX heuristic symbol extraction,
- import/dependency extraction,
- test discovery,
- risk rules,
- validation warnings.

Acceptance criteria:
- tests run locally without external services,
- tests are not dependent on the user's current working repository,
- tests use temp dirs/fixtures,
- tests are deterministic.

Verification:
- `python -m pytest` or the equivalent test command passes.

tasks.title: 28 - Add end-to-end test for init, index, and context
tasks.description:
Add an E2E test through the CLI or public functions.

Scenario:
1. create a temporary fixture repository,
2. run init,
3. adjust project.yaml/protected_paths if needed,
4. run index,
5. run context with task `Update context panel copy. Do not change matching algorithms.`,
6. verify artifacts.

Expectations:
- context_pack.md contains the task,
- it contains the relevant TSX screen/test,
- it contains invariants,
- it marks matching.py as risky/protected,
- it does not contain node_modules,
- opencode_prompt.md is created only when platform export is requested.

Acceptance criteria:
- test passes repeatedly,
- it does not require OpenCode,
- it verifies real files under `.vibecode/`.

Verification:
- `python -m pytest` includes this E2E test.

tasks.title: 29 - Add Windows path compatibility tests
tasks.description:
Verify that the tool works with Windows-style paths because the primary working environment is Windows/PowerShell.

Requirements:
- internally normalize relative paths to POSIX-style for JSON/MD outputs,
- do not write broken backslash escaping into JSON,
- accept roots such as `C:\path\to\example-repo` when running on Windows,
- do not treat a drive-letter colon as a YAML or path parsing problem.

Acceptance criteria:
- path normalizer has unit tests,
- JSON outputs are valid,
- documentation includes PowerShell examples.

Verification:
- unit test for the path normalizer,
- if CI is not Windows, at least test the pure normalization function with Windows-like strings.

tasks.title: 30 - Add command documentation and the first working scenario
tasks.description:
Update documentation for using the initial implementation.

Must include:
- what it can do,
- what it cannot do,
- local install/run instructions,
- init command,
- index command,
- map/inspect command,
- context command,
- OpenCode prompt export,
- explanation of the `.vibecode/` structure,
- human-maintained vs generated files,
- first scenario using `C:\path\to\example-repo` as an example, not a hardcoded dependency.

Acceptance criteria:
- a new developer can create a map for a fixture or real repository from the documentation,
- documentation specifically says OpenCode runtime comes later,
- documentation shows how to inspect output files.

Verification:
- manually walk through the quickstart on a temporary repository.

tasks.title: 31 - Add fail-safe behavior for empty or weak invariants
tasks.description:
When a project has no confirmed invariants, the tool must not ignore that fact.

Behavior:
- context pack may still be generated,
- it contains a visible warning: `Project has no confirmed invariants. Agent gets technical map but weak project rules.`,
- validation report emits WARN,
- map/inspect displays the warning.

Acceptance criteria:
- empty INVARIANTS.md is not a fatal error,
- warning is visible in validation and context pack,
- warning disappears when invariants exist.

Verification:
- test empty invariants,
- test filled invariants.

tasks.title: 32 - Prevent whole-file dumps in context packs
tasks.description:
The context pack must map and navigate; it must not push the entire repository into a prompt.

Requirements:
- context pack may list relevant files and short symbols,
- it must not paste full contents of long source files by default,
- any future excerpt must be short, explicitly labeled, and bounded by a limit,
- generated summaries must be truncated.

Acceptance criteria:
- no normal source file is fully embedded in context_pack.md,
- a test with a long file proves that the path/symbols appear, not the entire content,
- context pack remains agent-friendly.

Verification:
- unit or E2E test with a long fixture file and an assertion that the full content was not included.

tasks.title: 33 - Add clear user-facing errors without noisy stack traces
tasks.description:
The CLI must be usable. When something is missing, the primary output must not be a raw Python traceback.

Requirements:
- missing repo root => readable error,
- missing project.yaml during index/context => readable error suggesting `vibecode init`,
- invalid JSON/YAML => readable error with path and reason,
- permission denied => readable error,
- debug traceback only with `--debug`.

Acceptance criteria:
- common user errors have short messages and non-zero exits,
- `--debug` may show details,
- Ralph/OpenCode workers receive useful output, not chaos.

Verification:
- CLI tests for missing root/config and debug flag.

tasks.title: 34 - Prepare extension points for future OpenCode and Serena support without runtime implementation
tasks.description:
Prepare interfaces but do not implement a real runtime.

Enough for now:
- platform export registry: opencode currently only exports a prompt,
- code intelligence providers: symbol_map provider plus future Serena provider placeholder,
- context builder uses a provider interface instead of hardcoding everything in one file.

Non-goals:
- do not launch OpenCode,
- do not start an MCP server,
- do not install Serena.

Acceptance criteria:
- code is not a single monolithic 2000-line file,
- OpenCode prompt export is separated from the context-builder core,
- future Serena/OpenCode runtime support can be added as another module without rewriting the indexer.

Verification:
- code review confirms module boundaries are clear,
- tests still pass.

tasks.title: 35 - Final validation checklist for the initial implementation
tasks.description:
Perform the final validation pass.

Checklist:
- init works,
- index works,
- file_inventory.json is valid,
- repo_tree.md is readable,
- symbol_map.json is valid,
- dependency_map.json is valid,
- test_map.json is valid,
- entrypoints.md is created,
- risky_files.md is created,
- context_pack.md is created,
- opencode_prompt.md export works,
- human-maintained files are not overwritten,
- test suite passes,
- documentation is current,
- non-goals are not violated.

Acceptance criteria:
- final report lists changed files,
- it lists commands that were run,
- it lists warnings/limitations of the initial implementation,
- anything unfinished is named explicitly.

Verification:
- run the test suite,
- run the manual quickstart on a fixture repository,
- open generated artifacts and verify they make sense for an agent.
